name: top_k_selection_v1
description: K-sparse top-K adapter selection training data for the adapterOS LoRA router. Covers basic selection, tie-breaking via adapter index, single-dominant routing, uniform score distributions, K=1 single-adapter mode, and K=N full-mixture mode. All scores are Q15-quantized 6-decimal fixed-point values.

entries.notes: 1200 examples across 6 subcategories: basic_selection (200), tie_breaking (200), single_dominant (200), uniform_scores (200), k_equals_1 (200), k_equals_n (200)

provenance.review_notes: Synthetic dataset covering all top-K selection edge cases. Tie-breaking is deterministic via lower adapter index. Q15 quantization range is -1.0 to 0.9997.

evaluation_gates: Top-K selection is deterministic given identical gate scores
Tie-breaking always resolves to the lower adapter index
Non-selected adapters contribute zero weight to the inference pass
K=1 selects exactly one adapter regardless of score distribution
K=N activates all adapters, equivalent to full mixture routing
Single-dominant routing with K=3 still applies exactly K adapters
intent: Train the router to correctly identify and reason about top-K adapter selection across all common and edge-case score distributions, with emphasis on deterministic tie-breaking and the semantic implications of each K value.

subcategories.basic_selection: 16 adapters, K=3, clear distinct winners — step-by-step selection walkthrough
subcategories.tie_breaking: Two or more adapters share identical Q15-quantized scores — lower index wins
subcategories.single_dominant: One adapter scores >> all others; K=3 but routing is effectively single-adapter
subcategories.uniform_scores: All adapters score approximately equal; selection is index-dependent, entropy maximized
subcategories.k_equals_1: K=1 single-adapter mode; most selective, highest precision
subcategories.k_equals_n: K=N full-mixture mode; all adapters active, useful for ensemble and calibration
